title: LPSC votes 4-1 to approve Entergy gas plants for Meta
body: The Louisiana Public Service Commission voted 4-1 to approve Entergy Louisiana's proposal to build three new combined-cycle combustion turbine gas plants to power Meta's Hyperion campus. Commissioner Davante Lewis dissented, warning that Meta is contractually committed for only 15-20 years, leaving ratepayers potentially exposed to stranded costs after the contract period. The plants are part of "Project Everest," ten total gas plants dedicated to a single corporate customer.

title: Meta Hyperion water wells flagged by state officials
body: In July 2024, Louisiana officials warned Meta that nine of its proposed wells for the Hyperion campus posed environmental risks including acute water level decline, reduced water quality, and possible land subsidence. In May 2025, the state registered six of these wells, determining they "should not adversely affect water withdrawal from other registered water wells in the area or to the sustainability of the aquifer" — a determination disputed by water advocates.

title: Meta receives $3.3B in sales tax exemptions
body: Hyperion will be exempt from state and local sales and use taxes on data center equipment for 20 years. With Louisiana's combined state and local sales tax rate at 9.56%, and Meta projected to spend approximately $35 billion on GPU hardware, the exemption is worth an estimated $3.3 billion in foregone public revenue. The exemption covers the GPUs used to train and deploy AI models.

title: Entergy seeks $237M ITEP exemption for Meta gas plants
body: Entergy Louisiana filed for a property tax exemption worth an estimated $237 million over 10 years under the state's Industrial Tax Exemption Program for the new natural gas-fired power facilities it is building to serve Meta's Hyperion campus. The exemption would apply to infrastructure being built to serve a single private customer, with ratepayers bearing infrastructure costs.

title: Report: ratepayers could bear half the cost of Meta infrastructure
body: A June 2026 report found that Louisiana ratepayers could be left responsible for up to half the cost of the power infrastructure being built for Meta's datacenter operations, should Meta reduce its load or exit after its contract period. The report raises concerns about stranded asset exposure — a risk Commissioner Davante Lewis named in his LPSC dissent.

title: Amazon negotiates water limits for NW Louisiana campuses
body: Unlike Meta, Amazon agreed to limit its water use and to pay for its own power infrastructure as conditions of its $12 billion NW Louisiana datacenter investment in Caddo and Bossier Parishes. Advocates have cited these concessions as a model for future datacenter permit negotiations in the state.

title: "Digital Cancer Alley" — advocates warn of sacrifice zone pattern
body: Environmental and community advocates have drawn an explicit parallel between the emerging datacenter corridor and Cancer Alley — the industrial corridor between Baton Rouge and New Orleans where petrochemical facilities have concentrated pollution in Black and working-class communities for decades. A $10 billion Meta project is part of a broader $200 billion in total Big Tech investment across the South. Advocates warn that without strong resistance, new projects could cement Louisiana as a "digital sacrifice zone."
